Today you can help give that same moment to someone else.

No one like Julian Castro has ever been in the Oval Office. His grandmother immigrated here from Mexico when she was just 7 years old. She never got past the 4th grade, but she provided for her family by working as a maid and a housekeeper.

Now, her grandson is running for President of the United States. Against all odds, grassroots Democrats like YOU helped him qualify for the Democratic debates and sent him to the front of the pack of Democratic candidates.

name: Matt Innis
subject: Happy Holidays! Here's what we've been up to these last few weeks!
body:
Learn about upcoming events and how you can help the campaign! No images? [Click here](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-e-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-u/) [](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-l-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-r/) I want to wish everyone a very Merry Christmas and happy and safe Happy New Year. Our campaign has had a very busy couple of weeks. During these very busy weeks for people between the Thanksgiving and Christmas holidays, we decided to do as many media interviews as possible instead of asking people to spend their time going to events. Don't worry, we plan to be back in all of these towns for meet-and-greet events where you can ask me any question you want to ask!In the past two weeks we started with a tour of southeast Nebraska visiting with Nebraskans in Nebraska City and doing interviews with KNCY radio station and the Nebraska City News Press. Next we visited Auburn where I did an interview with the Nemaha County Herald. We followed that up going down to Falls City and doing radio and newspaper interviews with the Falls City Journal and KNTC radio station. The support by everyone in those towns was very encouraging and I continue to hear from people that they are unhappy with the incumbent senator we are running against. [Contact the Campaign to Have Matt Speak at Your Event!](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-l-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-y/) Following the southeast Nebraska tour we went to York where I did interviews with the York News-Times and KAWL radio. Later that day we went to Grand Island for a radio interview with KRGI, then went to Hastings for interviews with KSNB-TV and KHAS radio, as well as with the Hastings Tribune. Again, everywhere I go, people continue to tell me they are supporting our campaign and they do not support my opponent, Ben Sasse.After those two successful trips we went back to Sidney Nebraska to follow up on FOX News host Tucker Carlson's report on what happened to Sidney when Bass Pro bought Cabela's. We held a meet-and-greet with several people attending who took several more yard signs and they invited us back for a fundraiser. Sidney is a stronghold for the Innis for Senate campaign! I also visited some new companies who have come to Sidney since Bass Pro took their jobs away. Also in Sidney, we had a good meeting with several residents of Sidney I had not met before. Even though Sidney has been hit hard financially, we were humbled by their generosity and donations to our campaign. We need all of the financial support we can get to compete with the big Washington, DC money my opponent has! [Help Me Reach More Nebraskans - Donate Today!](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-l-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-j/) Next we went to Imperial where I did an interview with the Imperial Republican newspaper. After that we went to Benkelman where I interviewed with the Benkelman Post & News Chronicle. After Benkelman we went to McCook where I did an interview with KBRL radio station and the McCook Gazette.On the way from McCook to Holdrege, I stopped in Arapahoe to meet Lisa Fisher, who is a great supporter. I dropped off several yard signs with Lisa and will come back to give her 4' x 8' signs to put out in southwest Nebraska. If anyone in the McCook and Holdrege area needs a sign, let us know at our website and we'll have Lisa get you a sign. [Yes! I Need a Yard Sign!](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-l-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-t/) We finished our trip with an interview with the Holdrege Citizen Newspaper.As you can see, our campaign is willing to have me go everywhere in Nebraska and take the time to do interviews and meet with Nebraska voters, while my opponent continues to have a staffer drive around an empty bus to pretend he is visiting Nebraskans. The outpouring of support for our campaign is still humbling to me. We are giving out yard signs as fast as we have money to print them. If anyone has taken a yard sign, I would ask them to **donate to our campaign** so we can make even more signs to get my name out to all Nebraska voters. I have no doubt Ben Sasse, who gets 96% of his campaign funds from outside Nebraska, will have more money than us. But just getting my name out to Nebraska voters will let them know they have an option to vote for a real Republican who supports our farmers and ranchers and who supports President Trump. Again, I hope all of you have a very Merry Christmas with your family and friends, and very Happy New Year. I can't wait to get back on the campaign trail in January! I hope to see you all very soon on the campaign trail throughout Nebraska!Sincerely, **Matt Innis****Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate** **_Main Office_** Matt Innis for U.S. Senate
3434 O St
Lincoln, NE 68510 [](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-l-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-i/) | [](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-l-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-d/)| [](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-l-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-h/)| [](https://email.transformationmarketing.com/t/r-l-jhlyldkd-uktunkib-k/)

name: Dan Lipinski
subject: Happy Holidays from Congressman Lipinski
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Lipinski for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/NH2wkVSzpDtmDozFvfF3_z4rgeF2DFpxtcsoJK-8s4I=/TAXD-VLA4YSqeR7qK08vJ5j7tdmEOVPdmEbh8v4VZgg=)

Dear Peter:
This past weekend I spent some time as a bell ringer raising money for the Salvation Army outside of Jewel in Countryside. We live in tumultuous times and I heard a lot of comments on all sides about what has been going on in Washington. While there was a clear division on issues, I was buoyed by the "thank yous" I received for my work from so many people. But more importantly, my spirits were raised by the generosity of all those who dropped a donation into the bucket to help those less fortunate.
At this time of year, we are reminded that we should all work to put aside our differences and be thankful for what we've been given. No matter how you celebrate this holiday season, I hope that you have the opportunity to take some time with friends and loved ones to slow down a bit and reflect on the true gifts in life. And for those who are struggling at this time of year, I wish you comfort and peace.
From my family to yours, I wish you a blessed holiday season, Merry Christmas, and a happy and healthy New Year.
Dan

A Holiday Note from Team TNDP

‘Twas the year 2019 and all through the South
There were Democrats voting the GOP out
Corruption ran rampantly, too much to bear
Yet Republican leadership shrieked ‘We don’t care!’

Virginia came first, then Kentucky was next
Even Louisiana turned a few shades less red
“It’s all been a witch hunt, a partisan trap!”
The President tweeted, but nobody clapped

With transcripts released on Ukrainian matters
Impeachment proceedings were served on a platter
Fox News bore headlines ‘This Nonsense Won’t Last’
‘There was No Quid Pro Quo’ ‘Don’t Believe Democrats!’

Still opinions were shifting, polls started to show
As Donald obsessed with whose whistle was blown
Meanwhile Dems they all started to cheer
As a Blue 2020 was looking more clear

But who would they choose, who could pull off the trick
With candidates coming and going so quick?
“Healthcare, fair wages, I’ll beat him” they’d claim
So many to choose from, too many to name...

First Biden and Bernie
Then Warren and Pete
Could Booker or Amy
Or Yang pick up speed?
Is Bloomberg or Steyer
Or Castro the guy?
Whoever it is
The bar sure is set high

If this made you laugh, or cry with despair
Don’t waste your time thinking ‘life isn’t fair’
Our country’s divided, but don’t lose all hope
We’re stronger together, we just have to vote...

or Merry Christmas. We don't want to trigger anyone. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/c7aac2cc76cb/happy-holidays?e=c56804a14d)

| |

| |
# Happy Holidays!

We hope you're enjoying the holiday season! Denise and I send our regards and warm wishes.
---
|
---
|
---
We went for a smaller, more portable tree this year. _Rose_ merry Christmas, everyone!
|
---
| While this has certainly been another difficult year full of attacks on our democracy, I think we'd all like to take a few days off to reflect on the good things that have happened this last year and the last decade as they both come to a close (Yes, I prescribe to the belief that 2020, not 2021, is the first year of the new decade. I respect our potential differences in this heated debate.)

This is the decade I met all of you as you welcomed me home to Minnesota. This is the decade I met, fell in love with, and married my truly amazing wife, Denise. This is the decade I served my country in the Air Force and learned exactly what it means to be part of something much greater than myself. This is the decade we all proved that Congressional District 6 can be a competitive seat and is home to more progressive fighters than any of us knew.

I can't thank you enough for all the love and support you've shown over the years. Let's bring in the new year with renewed hope and determination in our hearts! Merry Christmas and happy holidays, everyone!

With cheer,

Ian Todd

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Happy Holidays!
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress
body:
Peter,

I wanted to take a moment to reach out and wish you all a joyous Holiday Season. Take some time this week to rest and enjoy the company of your loved ones. I am grateful for all of your support throughout this last year, and look forward to continuing fighting for you in Congress in the new year.

Warm regards,
Mike

* * *

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Today, I'm thinking about all that we've done together this past year -- our grassroots movement has made so much progress, and we are beginning to see real change already. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bd38/45c30650/11774bde7/66fac614/1581577925/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Today, I'm thinking about all that we've done together this past year -- our grassroots movement has made so much progress, and we are beginning to see real change already.

The plans that we've fought for have set a benchmark for what's possible in 2020 -- keeping issues that affect working families front and center in this race.

We've proven that it is possible to fund a presidential campaign without money from PACs, Washington lobbyists, or closed-door fancy fundraisers with mega-wealthy donors.

**The progress we've made this year has proven that when we dream big and fight hard together, we win. And if we continue to put in the work to lay the foundation now, I have no doubt in my mind that we'll win come 2020.**

You've played a role in all of this -- and I'm so glad to have you by my side in this fight. Thank you for everything you've done so far. Let's get ready to finish the year strong and head into 2020 ready to win.

-- Elizabeth

name: Congressman Al Green
subject: However you celebrate
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Keep Al Green in Congress |
body:
Folks,

I wanted to reach out personally to wish you a happy and healthy holiday season.

The holidays are a great opportunity to reflect on the year -- and 2019 has been a big one.

Over two years of hard work and movement-building led to the impeachment of the most corrupt president in modern American history. We also passed bills in the House to make Election Day a national holiday, close the background check loophole, raise the minimum wage to $15 an hour, provide a pathway to citizenship for DREAMers, and end gender-based discrimination.

All this has been made possible by your support. I can’t thank you enough for your righteous anger, commitment to justice, and willingness to speak out when it matters most.

But that’s enough politics for now -- happy holidays to you and yours!

With gratitude,

Congressman Al Green

Keep Al Green in Congress
P.O. Box 56761
Houston TX 77256-6761 United States

body:

| Happy holidays, and here's to a phenomenal 2020. I love the holidays, friend. It's a time of joy and a time you get to spend with loved ones. This year, I'll be with my wonderful family, my friends, and girlfriend. We'll enjoy a few nice meals, relaxed days, and best of all, _vegan eggnog_ (for anyone who's not down with dairy, it's a game-changer, check it out). **Nogs aside, this time of year always makes me reflect on what I 'm grateful for -- and this year, that's people like you. I don't know where I'd be right now without you.** Whether it's [**making a donation**](https://act.corybooker.%3D/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-em-december?refcode=em_x_20191224_CB_20191224-X-CB-EM1-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer="), [**signing up to volunteer**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0ca7/5fca0d19/7ba1dc22/291cfdf5/3303667340/VEsE/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://act.corybooker.com/page/s/get-involved?source=em_20191224-x-CB-EM1-actives") in early states, or even just sharing one of my posts on [**social media**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0ca7/5fca0d19/7ba1dc22/291cfdf4/3303667340/VEsF/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://twitter.com/CoryBooker"), I'm blown away by how many people have stepped up to be a part of this effort. Cory

If we miss it, our work to take back the Senate will be in jeopardy. We've got to hit our target to stay on track for the final quarter of the year (that's why our team launched this 48-hour fundraising push) but I'm still a little worried about where we're at right now. So, I'm asking: Can you pitch in before our fundraising deadline at midnight? Anything you can give will allow our campaign for U.S. Senate to get back on track for our final FEC deadline of the year. Chip in today. | | | Dear Friend,

As we wrap up 2019, I would like to thank you for being part of a special group who took a chance and supported me _early._ We have come so far in just six short months! **_None of this would have been possible without your help._**

If we continue building on our platform to **bring** **bold and effective leadership back to Pinellas** , I have no doubt we will be successful next November!

I look forward to working with you in 2020, and welcome the opportunity to visit with you individually to discuss this Congressional race and our path to victory next year.

May God bless you and your family this Christmas! I wish you a very peaceful, healthy and happy New Year!

Gratefully,

---

Friend: As president, Tom will make combating the climate crisis his **number one priority and declare a national emergency on day one.** It’s the biggest threat to our livelihoods, our children, our planet — and we have to start acting like it. Action on climate has been slow to the point of nonexistence because special interests and the politicians they’ve purchased have blocked progress at every turn. But it is inspiring to see the committed advocates, young people in particular, who are leading the fight for climate justice. **We must all come together to address this crisis head-on and build a stable future.** We can do this. And what’s more, America will be better for it. clean_body: | | | | Friend: As president, Tom will make combating the climate crisis his number one priority and declare a national emergency on day one. It’s the biggest threat to our livelihoods, our children, our planet — and we have to start acting like it. Action on climate has been slow to the point of nonexistence because special interests and the politicians they’ve purchased have blocked progress at every turn. But it is inspiring to see the committed advocates, young people in particular, who are leading the fight for climate justice. We must all come together to address this crisis head-on and build a stable future. We can do this. And what’s more, America will be better for it. Tom’s will transition our country to a green economy and create millions of good-paying jobs. It will redress environmental injustices and bring communities together to be the drivers of change. And it will restore our global leadership and ensure that America leads the world in the fight against climate change. Thank you for being a part of this important work. subject: Merry Christmas from True the Vote!
body:
May God fill your life with love, joy and peace this Holiday Season and throughout the New Year. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/1b0c9bfca885/merry-christmas-from-true-the-vote?e=ad8cba7e98)
---
| |
---
| |
#### We're coming to the end of an incredible year ... preparing for epic elections in the year to come. But now, in this season of celebration, it seems right to stop for a moment to recognize that through it all God was ever faithful to give us just enough light for the step we were on.

It's been a whirlwind, but we were never alone. And we never will be.

Wherever you are, no matter what personal journey you may be on, you are never alone.

Emmanuel! God is with each of us.

Good things will come in 2020. Miraculous things. The fight of the righteous is never fought alone. Emmanuel!

Merry Christmas! Happy Hanukkah! May God bless each of you - and may God continue to bless and keep the United States of America.

Onward -
_Catherine Engelbrecht and the whole team at True the Vote_
#

| Friend, From my family to yours, Bobby, Gabrielle and I want to to wish you Happy Holidays! No matter what holidays you celebrate, we are celebrating you, the community we have built together and the incredible year ahead of us. Our holiday message is one of family, both biological or chosen, friends and loved ones. Let this time be filled with joy and celebration knowing that the connections we have with each other are what the holidays are all about. We are so grateful for all of your support throughout the campaign. Your committment to family, friends and our state keeps hope alive. May the holiday spirit be with you and yours today and throughout the New Year! -The Hostlers

For decades, a small group of the richest families in the country have gobbled up more and more wealth, while the middle class has been hollowed out.

Now, the top 0.1% owns 20% of all the wealth in America -- up from 7% around 40 years ago.

Meanwhile, it's getting harder and harder for working families to cover costs like child care, housing, health care, and college.

**But Elizabeth has a plan to make our economy work for everyone -- paid for by a wealth tax on the top 0.1% -- and we have the power to make it happen.

[Check out our new interactive storytelling feature to see how it works and how we got here. Explore how the wealth gap was built, how Elizabeth's plan for a tax on wealth above $50 million will work, and how we'll be able to rebuild the middle class.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bd4d/45c30650/1174472f3/66fac2f5/1784182353/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTU0xQk9fTk9BX1VNVF9DXzIwMTkxMjI0X1YxUzNfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com/wealth-gap?source=EMM1BO_NOA_UMT_C_20191224_V1S3_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Inside this explainer, you'll see...
* An interactive chart to visualize the wealth gap between typical American families and the richest billionaires

* How much $50 million is, really -- including how many years you'd have to buy a $5 footlong every day before you spent $50 million

* Details on what the tax will pay for, including Universal Child Care and early education, universal free public technical, two-year, and four-year college, and canceling student loan debt for 42 million Americans

[**Scroll through this explainer, and you 'll have all these facts at your fingertips. Make sure you share this page far and wide, too, to help make the case for big, structural change!**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bd4d/45c30650/1174472f3/66fac2f5/1784182353/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTU0xQk9fTk9BX1VNVF9DXzIwMTkxMjI0X1YxUzNfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com/wealth-gap?source=EMM1BO_NOA_UMT_C_20191224_V1S3_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")

Team Warren

name: =?utf-8?Q?Juli=C3=A1n_Castro?=
subject: Happy Holidays, y'all!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| | | []()
---

**As millions of families around the nation gather with loved ones, I just wanted to share a quick note of gratitude.**

At the Castro home, it's Christmas Eve -- _Nochebuena_. It's a special time for us and we're thankful to be together. The holidays are a joyous occasion for many families, but they're also a difficult time for those in need.

My prayers are with the families of the 20 U.S. service members killed this year in Afghanistan. We can never repay our debt for their sacrifice, but we will always serve them to the best of our ability.

With fellow Catholics around the world, our family is celebrating the birth of Jesus and remembering the values He lived. Feeding the hungry. Caring for the sick. Defending the vulnerable. Welcoming the stranger. Treating everyone with compassion.

These values are not only shared by Catholics, but by all Americans -- people of every faith and those who don't follow religion. These values are what unite us around a bold, compassionate vision for the future.

Throughout this campaign, I've fought for the most vulnerable people, folks on the margins of society -- people experiencing homelessness, victims of gun violence, workers without rights, children in foster care, people with disabilities, migrants seeking refuge.

I believe in an America where everyone counts. I strive every day to elevate people with the least power, but the most potential. That's what motivates me to keep going.

The opportunity to run for president is a privilege, and I'm grateful for your support. By so many measures, we're doing something very special and truly unique in politics. This is a movement for justice, for equality, and basic human dignity for everybody.

You give me hope and optimism for the future. Out of these dark days, a new light is on the horizon.

To our Jewish neighbors, I wish you a Happy Hanukkah. Chag Sameach!

To those celebrating this week, Happy Kwanzaa!

**And from Erica, Carina, Cristi an, and Team Julian - today we wish you a Merry Christmas. **

May God Bless our troops and their families. And May God Bless our American family with peace and joy in the New Year.

Gratefully,
Julian

No one like Julian Castro has ever been in the Oval Office. His grandmother immigrated here from Mexico when she was just 7 years old. She never got past the 4th grade, but she provided for her family by working as a maid and a housekeeper.

Now, her grandson is running for President of the United States. Against all odds, grassroots Democrats like YOU helped him qualify for the Democratic debates and sent him to the front of the pack of Democratic candidates.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because we're relying on grassroots supporters to overcome our next big challenge:

Julian must be able to compete and build a movement that lasts for the long haul, and he's not taking a cent of corporate PAC money to do it. Will you be a part of this historic campaign?

**[DONATE](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc95d/3eb3e2fc/6999412a/744ece4b/1554162711/VEsC/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-em?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on&utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=4+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=20191224_em_castro_all_christmas&source=20191224_em_castro_all_christmas")**
Again, we know we send you a lot of emails. We hope you'll stick with us long-term. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, please let us know [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc95d/3eb3e2fc/6999412a/744ece4a/1554162711/VEsD/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=20191224_em_castro_all_christmas&source=20191224_em_castro_all_christmas"). If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. Please unsubscribe [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fc95d/3eb3e2fc/6999412a/744ece49/1554162711/VEsA/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=20191224_em_castro_all_christmas&source=20191224_em_castro_all_christmas"), and we'll make sure to process your request.
